name: geocoder (Python Geocoding Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Denis Carriere; underlying geocoding services may charge

auth.notes: Nominatim: free, no key. Google, Bing, Mapbox, HERE: API key required per provider

agent_readiness.notes: Unified geocoding API for multiple providers. import geocoder. g = geocoder.osm('San Francisco, CA'). g.lat, g.lng, g.city, g.country, g.address, g.json. Providers: osm (Nominatim), google, bing, mapbox, here, tomtom, opencage, arcgis, yahoo, yandex. Google: geocoder.google('address', key='API_KEY'). Reverse: geocoder.osm([37.78, -122.42], method='reverse'). Batch: for r in geocoder.osm('address', many=True). IP: geocoder.ip('me'). g.ok to check success. g.confidence for match quality. Cached: geocoder.google('address', session=session) with requests session.

gotchas: Project appears unmaintained — last meaningful update was 2018; consider geopy instead
Provider APIs change — some providers may have broken support
Rate limiting must be handled by caller — no built-in throttling
Nominatim usage policy: 1 req/sec max, no bulk geocoding
best_when: Quick prototyping with multiple geocoding backends via unified API
When geopy is already a dependency but geocoder's multi-provider API is preferred
IP-based geocoding for rough location detection
avoid_when: Production systems — project is effectively unmaintained; use geopy or direct provider SDKs
summary: geocoder provides a unified Python API across 20+ geocoding providers. geocoder.osm('address').lat, .lng, .city. Reverse geocoding. geocoder.ip('me'). Multiple providers: OSM, Google, Bing, Mapbox. MIT by Denis Carriere. EFFECTIVELY UNMAINTAINED since 2018 — use geopy for new projects. No type annotations. Rate limiting manual. Nominatim: 1 req/sec max policy.
